title: श्री गुरु पादुका स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गुरु पादुका स्तोत्रम् ॥

अनन्तसंसारसमुद्रतारनौकायिताभ्यां गुरुभक्तिदाभ्याम्।
वैराग्यसाम्राज्यदपूजनाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥१॥

कवित्ववाराशिनिशाकराभ्यां दौर्भाग्यदावाम्बुदमालिकाभ्याम्।
दूरीकृतानम्रविपत्तिताभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥२॥

नता ययोः श्रीपतितां समीयुः कदाचिदप्याशु दरिद्रवर्याः।
मूकाश्च वाचस्पतितां हि ताभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥३॥

नालीकनीकाशपदाह्रताभ्यां नानाविमोहादिनिवारिकाभ्याम्।
नमज्जनाभीष्टततिप्रदाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥४॥

नृपालिमौलिब्रजरत्नकान्तिसरिद्विराजत्झषकन्यकाभ्याम्।
नृपत्वदाभ्यां नतलोकपङ्क्ते: नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥५॥

पापान्धकारार्कपरम्पराभ्यां तापत्रयाहीन्द्रखगेश्वराभ्याम्।
जाड्याब्धिसंशोषणवाडवाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥६॥

शमादिषट्कप्रदवैभवाभ्यां समाधिदानव्रतदीक्षिताभ्याम्।
रमाधवाङ्घ्रिस्थिरभक्तिदाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥७॥

स्वार्चापराणामखिलेष्टदाभ्यां स्वाहासहायाक्षधुरन्धराभ्याम्।
स्वान्ताच्छभावप्रदसद्गुणाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥८॥

कामादिसर्पव्रजगारुडाभ्यां विवेकवैराग्यनिधिप्रदाभ्याम्।
बोधप्रदाभ्यां दृतमोक्षदाभ्यां नमो नमः श्रीगुरुपादुकाभ्याम्॥९॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
पादुकापञ्चरत्नमेतत् पठेद्यः प्रयतः सदा।
स गुरोः परमां भक्तिं लभते नात्र संशयः॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितं गुरुपादुकास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Guru Paduka Stotram ||

Anantasamsaarasamudra taaranaukayitaa bhyaam Gurubhaktidaabhyaam.
Vairaagyasaamraajyada poojanaabhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||1||

KavitvaVaaraashiNi shaakaaraabhyaam Daurbhaagyadavaambuda maalikaabhyaam.
Doorikritaanamra vipattitaabhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||2||

Nataa Yayoh Shreepatataam Sameeyuh Kadaachidapyaashu Daridravaryaah.
Mookaashcha Vaachaspatitaam Hi Taabhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||3||

Naaleekaneekaasha padaahritaabhyaam Naanaavimohaadinivaarikaa bhyaam.
Namajjanaabheeshtatatipradaa bhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||4||

Nripaalimaulibra jaratnakanti Saridviraajatjhasha kanyakaabhyaam.
Nripatvadaabhyaam Natalokapaangkteh Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||5||

Paapandhakaaraarkaparampara abhyaam Taapatrayaaheendrakha geshvaraabhyaam.
Jaadyabdhisamshoshana vaadavaabhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||6||

Shamaadishatkaprada vaibhavaabhyaam Samaadhi daanavrataDeekshitaa bhyaam.
Ramaadhavaanghrissthira bhaktidaabhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||7||

Svaarchaaparaanaam akhileshtadaabhyaam Svaahaa sahaayaakshadhur andharaabhyaam.
Svaantachchhabhaavaprada sadgunaa bhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||8||

Kaamaadisarpa vrajagaarudaabhyaam Vivekavairaa gya nidhipradaabhyaam.
Bodhapradaabhyaam Dritamokshadaabhyaam Namo Namah Shrigurupaadukaa bhyaam.||9||

|| Phalashruti ||
Paadukaapancharatna metat Pathedyah Prayatah Sadaa.
Sa Guroh Paramaam Bhaktim Labhate Naatra Samshayah.

|| Iti Shrimachhankaraacharyavirachitam Gurupaadukaastotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) अनन्त संसार-सागर को तारने की नौका-रूप, गुरुभक्ति प्रदान करने वाली, वैराग्य-साम्राज्य देने वाली पूजनीय श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

(2) कवित्व-समुद्र की चन्द्रमा-रूप, दुर्भाग्य-दावाग्नि की मेघमाला-रूप, नम्र भक्तों की विपत्तियों को दूर करने वाली श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

(3) जिनको नमस्कार करने से दरिद्र श्रीपति (लक्ष्मीपति) बन जाते हैं और मूक वाचस्पति (बृहस्पति) बन जाते हैं, उन श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

(4) कमल के समान चरणों द्वारा गृहीत, नाना प्रकार के मोह आदि को निवारित करने वाली, नमस्कार करने वालों की इच्छित वस्तुएँ प्रदान करने वाली श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

(5) राजाओं के मुकुटों के रत्नों की कान्ति-नदी में विराजमान मछली-रूप, भक्तों की पंक्ति को राजपद देने वाली श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को नमस्कार।

(6) पापरूपी अन्धकार के लिए सूर्य-परम्परा-रूप, तीन तापों के लिए गरुड़-रूप (सर्पों का नाशक), जड़ता-समुद्र को सुखाने वाली वडवाग्नि-रूप श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को नमस्कार।

(7) शम आदि षट्क (छः सम्पत्तियाँ) प्रदान करने वाली, समाधि-दान-व्रत में दीक्षित, रमाधव (विष्णु) के चरणों में स्थिर भक्ति देने वाली श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को नमस्कार।

(8) अपनी पूजा करने वालों की सभी इच्छाएँ पूर्ण करने वाली, स्वच्छ भाव और सद्गुण प्रदान करने वाली श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को नमस्कार।

(9) काम आदि सर्पों के समूह के लिए गरुड़-रूप, विवेक-वैराग्य का भण्डार प्रदान करने वाली, बोध और शीघ्र मोक्ष देने वाली श्री गुरु पादुकाओं को नमस्कार।
benefits: Shankaracharya's devotional hymn to the sacred sandals of the Guru
Worshipping Guru's Padukas removes all sins like the sun removes darkness
Even the poorest become wealthy and the mute become eloquent
Ideal for daily recitation and Guru Purnima
description: Guru Paduka Stotram — श्री गुरु पादुका स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Dattatreya. Attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to shankaracharya' devotional hymn to the sacred sandals of the Guru and grant Viveka (discrimination), Vairagya (dispassion), and Moksha. It is also said to bestow Shama-Dama-Uparati-Titiksha-Shraddha-Samadhana (six virtues). For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima. The text contains 9 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Guru Paduka Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Guru Paduka Stotram by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Guru's sandals for devotion, wisdom and liberation.
relatedSearches: Guru Paduka Stotram lyrics Sanskrit Devanagari PDF
Nine verse guru paduka stotram meaning

seoFaqs.question: What is the Guru Paduka Stotram and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Guru Paduka Stotram is the celebrated nine-verse Sanskrit hymn composed by Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE), saluting the sacred sandals (padukas) of his Guru — traditionally identified as Sri Govinda Bhagavatpadacharya, the disciple of Gaudapada. The hymn is also known as Paduka Pancharatnam (the five jewels of the padukas) in some recensions. Each verse is a pair of compound epithets describing how the Guru's padukas accomplish a specific transformation in the disciple — they are the boat across the infinite ocean of samsara, the moon over the ocean of poetic inspiration, the Garuda for the serpents of misfortune, the sun-cluster destroying the darkness of sin, the underwater fire drying the ocean of dullness — closing each verse with Namo Namah Sri Guru Padukabhyam. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya complete works; Sringeri Sharada Peetham; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are the Guru's padukas (sandals) worshipped in Hindu tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Hindu Guru-shishya tradition, the Guru's padukas (wooden sandals) symbolise the Guru's continual presence and grace even when he is physically absent or after his samadhi. They are considered the paramount tirtha — the sacred object that embodies the Guru's shakti. The mathas of the four Adi Shankara amnaya peethams (Sringeri, Puri, Dvaraka, Jyotirmath) and the Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham preserve the original padukas of Adi Shankara and successor acharyas as living objects of worship. The padodaka (water that has touched the Guru's feet) is the most sacred prasada. Worshipping the padukas is considered equivalent to worshipping the Guru himself, since the entire Guru-shakti flows through them. (Sources: Sringeri Sharada Peetham guides; Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham; Hindupedia Guru parampara.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the verse 'Anantasamsara Samudra Tara Naukayitabhyam' mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse describes the Guru's padukas as Anantasamsara-samudra-tara-naukayitabhyam (the boat that ferries one across the boundless ocean of samsara), Guru-bhakti-dabhyam (granters of devotion to the Guru), and Vairagya-samrajya-da-pujanabhyam (the worship of which bestows the sovereign empire of dispassion). This single verse encapsulates the threefold gift of true Guru-upasana: vehicle for liberation (cross samsara), the very bhakti to recognise the Guru, and vairagya so total that it is called a samrajya (kingdom). The verse models how subsequent verses each pair two transformative powers — adding viveka, jnana, sama-dama, moksha — culminating in verse 9's Bodhapradabhyam Drita-mokshadabhyam. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya Guru Paduka Stotram; Sringeri commentary tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Guru Paduka Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is the prescribed weekday since it is sacred to all gurus and to Brihaspati (the deva-guru). The supreme occasion is Guru Purnima — Ashadha Shukla Purnima (July full moon) — celebrated as the birth of Veda Vyasa (Vyasa Purnima) and the ideal day for Guru-puja. Devotees who have a living Guru perform paduka-puja by symbolically washing the Guru's padukas (or his picture's feet) with milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar (panchamrita), then recite the stotram. Those without a physical Guru visualise Adi Shankara's or their ishta-guru's padukas. Daily recitation at brahma-muhurta is recommended for sadhakas in the Sri Vidya, Smarta, and Advaita traditions. (Sources: Drikpanchang Guru Purnima; Sringeri Sharada Peetham; Hindupedia Guru worship.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Guru Paduka Stotram grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares: Padukapancharatnam-etat pathed-yah prayatah sada, sa guroh paramam bhaktim labhate naatra samshayah — 'whoever recites this Paduka Pancharatnam diligently and constantly attains the supreme devotion to the Guru, of this there is no doubt.' The verses themselves promise: even the poorest become wealthy and the mute become eloquent (verse 3), removal of all moha-vimoha (delusions, verse 4), royal recognition (verse 5), destruction of all paapa, removal of the three taapas, dissolution of jadya (dullness, verse 6), the six wealth of shama-dama-uparati-titiksha-shraddha-samadhana (verse 7), accomplishment of all desires (verse 8), and viveka-vairagya-bodha-moksha (verse 9). The hymn is called the master-key to all of Adi Shankara's Vedanta. (Sources: Adi Shankara phalashruti; Sringeri Peetham; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Adi Shankara and his Guru Govinda Bhagavatpada?
seoFaqs.answer: Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE) sought initiation from Govinda Bhagavatpadacharya at the latter's hermitage in a cave on the banks of the Narmada at Omkareshwar (Madhya Pradesh). According to the Shankara-vijaya hagiographies (Madhaviya, Anandagiri, Chitsukha versions), young Shankara stopped a flooding Narmada from entering Govindapada's cave by miraculously containing the river in his kamandalu — recognising his disciple's siddhi, Govinda Bhagavatpada gave him sannyasa-diksha and mahavakya-upadesha. Govinda was himself the disciple of Gaudapadacharya, author of the Mandukya Karika. This sampradaya-parampara — Gaudapada → Govinda Bhagavatpada → Adi Shankara — is the foundation of Advaita Vedanta and the four amnaya peethams. The Guru Paduka Stotram is Shankara's eternal salute to this lineage. (Sources: Madhaviya Shankara Vijaya; Sringeri Peetham; Hindupedia Adi Shankara.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Guru Paduka Stotram differ from Guru Stotram and Guru Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are central to Guru-bhakti but differ in scope. The Guru Paduka Stotram (9 verses, Adi Shankara) is a focused meditation on the transformative power of the Guru's padukas — most popular short Guru-stuti. The Guru Stotram (often the 11-verse Gurur Brahma Gurur Vishnuh from Skanda Purana's Guru Gita) is the universal Guru-vandana hymn known to every Hindu — used as the opening invocation before any spiritual practice. The Guru Gita is a much longer 352-verse dialogue between Shiva and Parvati from the Skanda Purana — read as parayana especially during Guru Purnima fortnight, considered the supreme scripture on Guru-tattva. Daily sadhana usually opens with the Guru Stotram, then recites the Paduka Stotram, with Guru Gita reserved for Thursdays and Guru Purnima. (Sources: Skanda Purana Guru Gita; Adi Shankara stotras; Stotra Nidhi.)
